observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Inventory Committee and the GSO to conduct a complete physical inventory of
all the Municipality’s property and correspondingly prepare and submit to the
Audit Team the required inventory report thereon;

b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and GSO to reconcile their reports and investigate
discrepancies noted between the inventory report and accounting and property
records; and

c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and GSO to make the necessary adjustments to
reconcile the PPE accounts.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a. coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer to determine the details
of the demolished structures under Investment Property-Building account and
subsequently make necessary adjustment to correct the value of the said
account; and

b. prepare a reclassification entry for the misclassified properties recorded under
Investment Property to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account
to present the correct balances of affected accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation of both Cash-Local
Treasury and CIB-LCCA accounts to establish the correct balance of the said
accounts. Also, necessary adjustments, if there is any, should be effected at once
to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records.

We also reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a. facilitate the investigation on the negative balance of the PNB accounts
amounting to P1,595,151.27 and make formal representation to the PNB
Manager to identify the valid reconciling items. Thereafter make necessary
adjusting entries to correct the balance of the said bank accounts; and

henceforth,

b. ensure compliance with Section 181(c) of the GAAM. Volume I to avoid
existence of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of errors if needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. implementing Division to check the Memorandum of Agreement if they are still
on the timeline and coordinate with the Source Agency so the Municipality can
adopt measures or catch-up plan to fast track the implementation of PPAs; and

b. OIC-Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal
Accountant, to remit to the BTR/Source Agencies all the unused balance of
funds pursuant to the cited regulations, so that the amount could be
immediately used for other projects/programs of the National Government
and to relieve the Municipality from any accountability/liability for the said
unused funds. Should there be a need for the unspent funds, the
Municipality should request from the source agencies for authority/approval
to use the excess amounts for other purposes or projects.

We strongly reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from
and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on
the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial
statements; and henceforth,

b. conduct periodic reconciliation of Municipal accounts with its reciprocal
accounts in the Province's books to avoid the accumulation of
discrepancies between two records.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

a. direct the GSO to prepare the corresponding IIRUP for the identified
unserviceable properties;

b. create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible in
appraising the scrap/salvage value of those unserviceable properties;

c. dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD
No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration; and

d. instruct the GSO to submit the necessary documents such as list of
unserviceable documents, pictures of disposed equipment, deed of
donation if donated to other agency or the Official Receipt from sale of the
unserviceable PPE item/s to the Accounting Office, as basis of the latter to
drop the unserviceable properties from books of accounts in accordance
with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the OIC-Municipal
Accountant the abstract of real property tax collections to facilitate the
remittance of barangay shares from RPT collections of the Municipality; and

b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit the barangay
shares from Real Property Tax collections within five days after the end of each
quarter in compliance with Section 271 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to:

a. analyze and determine the payables without valid documentation and those
that have been outstanding for two years or more, and subsequently request
an authority to the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the same; and

b. prepare the necessary journal entry to revert the said outstanding obligations
to Government Equity pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445 so that the
Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to reflect the fair value of the Breeding Stocks in
the financial statements and recognize gain from changes in fair value less
cost to sell of the biological assets due to physical change based on the
periodic appraisal of breeding stocks to be conducted by the Municipal
Agriculturist, pursuant to IPSAS 27;

b. Municipal Agriculturist to conduct close and regular monitoring of the status
of the remaining existing breeding stocks, so as to transfer, birth and death of
animals could be fully accounted for and provide the OIC-Municipal
Accountant with the monitoring report and the periodic appraisal of breeding
stocks; and

c. concerned officials to exert efforts in finding relevant documents such as the
report from the farmers/beneficiaries regarding the death of the cattle, if any,
the report on the investigation conducted and action taken by the Municipality
on the matter to support the request for relief from accountability to be filed to
the Commission on Audit (COA).

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. immediately remitto the GSIS the amount of P280,814.74, which pertains to the
unremitted premium contributions consisting both of the Municipality’s and
employees’ shares and loan payments; and henceforth,

b. strictly comply with Sections 6(b) and 7 of RA No. 8291.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to:

a. submit the remaining unsubmitted reports to the Audit team;

b. ensure that all financial reports and their supporting documents are submitted
to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular
Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the
official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground
for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have
complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be
instituted against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines); and

c. determine the cause/s of the delay in the submission of the accounts of the
Municipality to the Audit Team and find solutions thereto.

a. OIC-Municipal Accounting Office and the assigned General Services Officer to conduct thorough reconciliation of their respective records between the accounting records and the submitted Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and prepare/submit the required report thereon to the Audit Team; b. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property and prepare/submit the required report thereon to the Audit Team; c. General Services Officer to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to account for all unserviceable and obsolete items which will come from previously taken up in the books as assets to facilitate the completion of all the documentary requirements needed, so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the accounts; and d. General Services Officer and the Property Officer to ensure that Property Cards and PARs are completely maintained and updated to facilitate an orderly physical count of all the Municipality’s properties and determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment was issued and issue the corresponding PAR for each property to pinpoint accountability and responsibility.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Team will continue to make all possible solutions to improve the process of physical inventory. This is with the presence of the assigned representative from the Accounting Office. GSO still waiting for the accumulated depreciation and impairment losses to come from the Accounting Office. The Property Cards and PARs are completely maintained and at present, they are on the process of renewal/updating the PARs for all the officials, municipal employees to whom the equipment was issued and issue the corresponding PAR for each property to pinpoint accountability and responsibility.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of respective records between the OIC-Municipal Accountant and GSO is ongoing. Upon, completion of reconcilation, necessary adjustments will be done.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accounting Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation of both Cash-Local Treasury and CIB-LCCA accounts to establish the correct balance of the said accounts. Also, necessary adjustments, if there is any, should be effected at once to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records. b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to write a letter to investigate the details of the negative balance of PNB bank accounts and make formal representation to the PNB Manager to identify the valid reconciling items. Thereafter prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the balance thereof. c. Accounting Office to prepare and submit the BRS for the LBP bank account numbers 1141-0566-85 and 1142-1045-35; and d. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to complete the reconciliation of their records for both Cash-Local Treasury and CIB-LCCA accounts and henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the two records to ensure accuracy in recording.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will coordinate with the depository bank for the prompt release of the monthly bank statements or consider enrolling the Municipality to online banking facility (weAccess of Landbank of the Phils.) which is being access by Accounting and Treasury Office. The Accounting office submitted the reports for the listed bank accounts. The Municipality provides the details of the negative balance. Retrieval of the documents pertaining to the PNB account is ongoing so as to effect necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance thereof. The OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer have continuously effected proper reconciliation of their records.
observations.reason: Continuous reconciliation between the records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer is ongoing. The negative balance in PNB account is being reconciled and necessary adjustments will be effected upon retrieval of documents.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Human Resource Officer to provide additional engineering personnel to ensure the preparation of the complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects prior to 2015 and the detailed Road Map of the Municipality; b. Municipal Engineer to provide the General Services Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost segregation of road infrastructure components of completed road projects to facilitate the preparation of the report on Local Road Network; c. General Services Officer to prepare the Report on Local Road Network in proper format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 and Road Map of the Municipality and coordinate with MPDC for identification of the road map of the municipality; and d. OIC-Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the corresponding reports from the Municipal General Services and the Municipal Engineering Offices make the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the previous years' Local Road Network in order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer through the Property Section has already furnished the Engineering Office the prescribed form to be used in the preparation of report on Local Road Network and coordinate with MPDC for the identification of the road map of the municipality and to inform the Accounting Dept, to officially furnish the Engineering Dept, as well as the Property Section of GSO of the Official copy of registry list of Infrastructure Assets subject to detailed cost segregation. The Accounting office will record what should be returned back to the books once the reports were submitted.
observations.reason: The Engineering Office submitted the Report on Local Road Network however complete description and detailed cost segregation were not included in the prescribed period of 2018 to 2020. The required reports that should have been prepared and submitted by the concerned officials to the OIC-Municipal Accountant were still not provided; as a result, the OIC-Municipal Accountant has no basis to draw the necessary adjusting journal entry.

observations.recommendation: Prepare SSMI for the Inventory Held for Distribution, Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory and submit copy of the said document to OIC-Municipal Accountant. Thereafter, OIC-Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these account are based on the SSMI. Likewise, require him to validate and update records relative to the issued supplies and materials, make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements and promote better control of its receipts and issuances.
observations.managementAction: Management is preparing the SSMI for the Inventory Held for Distribution/Office Distribution and Drugs and Medicines Inventory and submitting the document to OIC-Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording the purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account.
observations.reason: The Supply Management is preparing the SSMI and the process of validation and adjustment is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to: a. coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer to determine the details of the unaccounted, demolished and damaged structures under Investment Property-Building account and thereafter analyze the details and information gathered for proper reclassification of said account to Markets account together with the Investment Property-Land account to Land account, both under Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality to present the correct balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will coordinate the details of the unaccounted and demolished properties. Reclassification will be effected once the necessary reconciliation is done. The Municipality of Tanay already have the necessary documents and the Municipality will effect the reclassification and adjustment in the books of Management.
observations.reason: They are still looking for the necessary details of the unaccounted properties so as to effect the adjustment in the books. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2 Page No. 39, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: a. coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments could be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements; and henceforth, b. exercise due professional care in the recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts be the same at all times.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Accountant, stated that the Municipal Accounting Office and the Provincial Accountant has been continuously coordinating to reconcile the reciprocal accounts of Due to and from LGUs.
observations.reason: Continuous coordination ongoing; differences still exist between Municipality and Provincial records. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5 Page No. 46, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to reflect the fair value of the Breeding Stocks in the financial statements and recognize gain from changes in fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets due to physical change based on the periodic appraisal of breeding stocks to be conducted by the Municipal Agriculturist, pursuant to IPSAS 27; b. Municipal Agriculturist to conduct close and regular monitoring of the status of the remaining existing breeding stocks, so as to transfer, birth and death of animals could be fully accounted for and provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the monitoring report and the periodic appraisal of breeding stocks; and c. concerned officials to exert efforts in finding relevant documents such as the report from the farmers/beneficiaries regarding the death of the cattle, if any, the report on the investigation conducted and action taken by the Municipality on the matter to support the request for relief from accountability to be filed to the Commission on Audit (COA).
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the Municipal Agriculturist, stated that the monitoring report was already submitted by the Municipal Agriculturist to the OIC-Municipal Accountant. Also, Management has requested assistance from Provincial Veterinarian to comply with the audit recommendations but as of date no response from the Office of the Provincial Veterinarian has been received by Management.
observations.reason: Management has requested assistance from Provincial Veterinarian; no response yet from Provincial Veterinarian to validate and effect adjustments.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to hasten, reconcile and trace the amount of P30,038.85 with the concerned agencies and make the necessary dispositions on the said balances so that appropriate disposition on the said balances can be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Accountant, stated that the OIC-Municipal Accountant is determining the details of the unremitted amounts since when they coordinated with the GSIS in the past years, a certification was provided by the latter that the Municipality has no outstanding balance to be remitted. As per the books, the amount of P280,814.74 pertains to non-moving amount withheld during 1980s to 2000s and the details of which were not fully established, Management agreed to coordinate with GSIS for the said balance.
observations.reason: Investigation ongoing; establishment of details and reconciliation with GSIS required; historical non-moving items complicate adjustment.

observations.recommendation: OIC-Human Resource Officer to publicize and open the vacant positions to all qualified applicants for the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant positions and consider selection and appointment in accordance with civil service rules.
observations.managementAction: The LGU Tanay posted the vacant position of Municipal Accountant on the Municipality’s website. As for the appointment of the Municipal Treasurer, three candidates for examination were endorsed; however, no action has been done. The Municipality has already added staff that will be in charge on the monthly reports and the Municipality is coordinating with Civil Service for the examination of candidates.
observations.reason: The LGU posted the vacancy for Municipal Accountant; appointment of Municipal Treasurer not yet completed; examination of candidates pending.

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Mayor consider assigning or hiring additional personnel to the Accounting Office; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that the monthly reports and their supporting documents are complete and regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed by COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Accountant, stated that due to lack of manpower and faulty scanning machine, they were not able to submit the financial reports and its corresponding documents to the Audit Team on time. However, they agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Delays due to lack of manpower and faulty scanning equipment; actions being taken to submit and comply.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of BGCM funds as provided under LBC No. 125 and to ensure that the copy of the utilization report is submitted to the concerned agencies as well as to the Audit Team for verification; and b. concerned department to update the Municipality’s website and make the necessary posting as part of their compliance with public disclosure requirements.
observations.managementAction: Said Utilization Reports were submitted to the concerned NGA. Series of notices were forwarded thru email to DBM Region IV-A for the months of May, June, July and August 2021, and are duly acknowledged. Concerned departments updated the posted Municipality’s website and made necessary postings as part of compliance.
observations.reason: Utilization reports were submitted and acknowledged; website postings updated.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately remit the cash refund of the six ineligible beneficiaries totaling P39,000.00 to the DSWD Field Office IV-A so that it can be given to another families who are in need of the cash assistance from the Government; and b. MSWDO Head to create a team to revalidate the SACUP for the records of those who refund, update it and resubmit the same to DSWD for proper monitoring and validation.
observations.managementAction: OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant submitted a copy of Official Receipt No. 9297385 dated June 22, 2021 amounting to P39,000.00 as a proof of the remittance to DSWD Field Office IV-A. MSWDO created a team and provided the final list to DSWD for validation.
observations.reason: Remittance made and proof submitted; MSWDO validated and coordinated with DSWD.

observations.recommendation: OIC-Human Resource Officer to comply with the mandatory staffing requirements of LDRRMO as prescribed by RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No. 2014-01 by filling up plantilla positions to ensure effective and efficient implementation of disaster related activities and projects.
observations.managementAction: The LGU Tanay posted the vacant positions and filled LDRMO III, LDRMO II and LDRMO I positions. The LDRMO positions were filled accordingly.
observations.reason: Positions for LDRMO posted and filled; staffing requirements complied.

observations.recommendation: OIC-LDRRMO to reassess the Municipality's storage situation in terms of stockpiling and establish agreements with private suppliers/retailers (e.g., supermarket) for prompt provision of stocks in case of emergency to avoid large stockpiles that may spoil or expire.
observations.managementAction: LGU Tanay has executed a Memorandum of Agreement with Eastern Tanay Supermarket dated May 24, 2017 so that stocks needed can be easily provided as necessary, and to ensure that goods to be distributed are new and not near expiration.
observations.reason: Advised by Evaluators to not have large stockpiles; MOA executed with supermarket; stocks to be procured as necessary.

observations.recommendation: a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made; and b. Bids and Awards Committee and all those involved in the procurement process to strictly comply with the procedural and documentary requirements for government procurements.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were submitted and received by COA on January 22, 2021, but it was mistakenly attached to the liquidation report. Management assured that those concerned in procurement process will comply with procedural and documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were submitted to COA; procurement procedures reinforced.

observations.recommendation: a. Review the GAD Plan and Budget to determine the PPAs that are responsive to gender gaps or issues faced by their employees, constituents and clients; and b. Utilize the GAD Fund solely for PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on COA Circular No. 2014-01 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the GAD-TWG Vice Chairman, stated that due to the COVID-19 Pandemic, the implementation of the GAD PPAs was not fully implemented. Moreover, they ensured that they will monitor the implementation of the PPAs for the 2022 GPB and all upcoming GAD Plans. They reasoned out that due to the late review conducted by the DILG-Provincial Office, they assumed that the Janitorial and Security Services PPAs are allowed GAD PPAs since it was also assessed by Management using the HGDG assessment tool. Nevertheless, Management assured that for the coming years, they will charge the Janitorial and Security Services in the Municipality's regular budget.
observations.reason: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic and late review by DILG-Provincial Office, several PPAs were not implemented; further monitoring planned for 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider assigning or hiring additional personnel to the Accounting Office to ensure the timely submission of accounts of the Municipality to the Audit Team pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submission of the disbursement and collection reports including their supporting (i.e., original copies of disbursement/journal entry vouchers and its supporting documents, duplicate copies of the official receipt and validated deposit slips) for CY 2020 was still being done way beyond the period prescribed by the above regulations. The delay ranges from 39 to 279 days. The OIC-Municipal Accountant explained that during the year an additional staff was assigned to their Office who will help in the scanning of the documents since the Municipality is currently implementing a "paper-free office". However, there was still delay in the submission of the documents due to the limited number of staffs reporting to work due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In addition, some of the accounting personnel were helping in the relief goods distribution or social amelioration program pay-out.
observations.reason: Limited number of staff reporting to work due to COVID-19 pandemic; staff re-assigned to relief distribution; scanning/paper-free transition causing delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to open the vacant position to all qualified applicants to be able to fill the position of Municipal Accountant that will take charge of both the Accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality to help ensure the proper stewardship of public funds pursuant to Section 474 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management. No reason was given for the non-implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mavor
direct the:
a. OIC-Mumicipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to conduct thorough
reconciliation of their respective records in order to determine the details and
causes of discrepancies between the balances per accounting records and
physical count and make the necessary adjustments to present the true value
of PPE accounts in the financial statements;
b. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory of
all the Municipality’s property in order to confirm its existence or condition and
prepare/submit the required report thereon to the Audit Team.
c. General Services Officer to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to account for all unserviceable and obsolete
items previously taken up in the books as assets and to facilitate the completion
of all the documentary requirements needed, so that they may be properly
disposed of and dropped from the accounts. Moreover, the disposal of property
shall be made in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27,
1989 and
d. General Services Officer and the Property Officer to ensure that Property Cards
and PARs are completely maintained and updated to facilitate an orderly
physical count of all the Municipality’s properties. Also, determine the
officials/employees to whom the equipment was issued and issue the
corresponding PAR for each property to pinpoint accountability and
responsibility. Lastly, assure that PARs are renewed at least once in every three
years oir every time there is a change in custodian of the property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to immediately record the transactions in the
Municipality’s books to present the correct balances of the CIB-LCCA and other
affected accounts at any given time;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the depository bank forthe prompt
release of the monthly bank statements or consider enrolling the Municipality to
online banking facility for easy access of bank statements to facilitate the timely
preparation of the BRS;
c. Accounting Office to prepare and submit the BRS for the LBP bank account
numbers 1141-0566-85 and 1142-1045-35;
d. OIC-Municipal Accountant to investigate the details of the negative balance of
PNB bank accounts and make formal representation to the PNB Manager to
identify the valid reconciling items. Thereafter, prepare the necessary journal
entries to adjust/record all valid book reconciling items to present the correct
balances of the said bank accounts; and
e. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to complete the
reconciliation of their records for both Cash-Local Treasury and CIB-LCCA
accounts. Thereafter, conduct periodic reconciliation of the two records to
ensure accuracy in recording. Also, necessary adjustments, if there is any,
should be effected at once to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between
the two records.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Human Resource Officer to provide additional engineering personnel to
ensure the preparation of the complete description and detailed cost
segregation of road components of completed road projects and the detailed
Road Map of the Municipality;
b. Municipal Engineer to provide the General Services Officer and the OIC-
Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost
segregation of road components of completed road projects of the Municipality
for years prior to 2015 to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and
proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts;
c. Generali Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare
the Report on Local Road Network in proper format as prescribed by COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 and Road Map of the
Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of
the Municipality's road network system; and
d. OIC-Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the corresponding required reports
from the Municipal General Service and the Municipal Engineering Offices make
the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the previous years’ Local
Road Network in order to present the correct balance of the Local Road
Networks account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to
prepare SSMI for the Inventory Held for Distribution, Office Supplies Inventory and
Drugs and Medicines Inventory and submit copy of the said document to OIC-
Municipal Accountant Thereafter, OIC-Municipal Accountant to record all
purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account/s and
ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are based on the SSMI. Likewise,
require him to validate and update records relative to the issued supplies and
materials, make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the
expense and Inventory accounts in the financial statements by debiting Expense
account and crediting the corresponding Inventory account, and promote better
control of its receipts and issuances.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer to
determine the details of the unaccounted, demolished and damaged structures
under Investment Property-Building account; and thereafter, analyze the details and
information gathered for proper reclassification of said account to Markets account
together with the Investment Property-Land account to Land account, both under
Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality to present the correct balances
of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. make a detailed review of all the recorded reciprocal accounts and coordinate
with the Provincial Accounting Office in order to determine the cause/s of the
unreconciled amounts of P2,513,011.30 and P12,717,465.51 between the
recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs of the Municipality and the
Rizal Provincial Government. Thereafter, make the necessary
adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the true balances
of the affected accounts; and henceforth,
b. exercise due professional care in the recording and classifying the
Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the
reciprocal accounts be the same at all times.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
reflect the fair value of the Breeding Stocks in the financial statements and
recognize gain from changes in fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets
due to physical change based on the periodic appraisal of breeding stocks to be
conducted by the Municipal Agriculturist, pursuant to IPSAS 27.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above
subject accounts and remit whatever the balance due to the agencies concerned
to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the
employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium
contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in
accordance with the cited regulations; and
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to hasten, reconcile and trace the amount of
P30,038.85 with the concerned government agencies so that appropriate
disposition on the said balances can be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Human Resource
Management Officer (HRMO) to publicize and open the vacant positions to all
qualified applicants for the Municipal Treasurer who will take charge of the
disbursement of all local government funds and such other funds the custody of
which may be entrusted to him/her by law or other competent authority pursuant to
Section 470 of RA No. 7160, and Municipal Accountant who will perform both the
accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality to help ensure the proper
stewardship of public funds pursuant to Section 474 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider assigning or hiring additional
personnel to the Accounting Office; and direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
ensure that the monthly reports and their supporting documents are complete and
regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed by the
mentioned regulations.

observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. reconcile their bank account records regularly, and effect the necessary adjustment to avoid accumulation of staled outstanding checks which should have been adjusted either as a liability account or a prior year's adjustment provided that an evidence should be presented to support the same;
b. verify and evaluate the accuracy and correctness of the reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation statements, support the bank statements, particularly the unrecorded disbursements and deposits; and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to come up with the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank account; and
c. closely coordinate with each other with regard to the accumulation of staled outstanding checks which should have been adjusted either as a liability account or a prior year's adjustment provided that an evidence should be presented to support the same.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office will exert more effort in reconciling the books of their respective records and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records; The Municipal Office will coordinate with concerned Offices regarding the validity of unclaimed and staled checks if those are still valid obligation, so that liability account will be set up will not be bloated.
Reconciliation between Accounting and Treasury Office is ongoing and if there are transactions found that was not recorded, necessary adjustments will be made. As for the closed account with negative balance, the OIC-Municipal Accountant will write a letter to PNB to determine the details and make necessary adjustments. Also, the OIC-Municipal Accountant attached a copy of the LBP bank statement for SEF account since the amount of the bank confirmation balance was different from the ending of their bank statement.
Municipal Treasurer also committed to address the observations and to comply for the right records by requesting the Municipal Mayor for additional employees to address admin services and recordings and reconciliation of accounts. Treasurer also instructed the personnel who handle the cashbook to do monthly reconciliation.
observations.reason: The reason for discrepancy is solely due to timing period; Treasury Office record in the cashbook all checks issued, while in the Accounting Office, it is being recorded as Disbursement once the check was released to the payee and forwarded to Accounting Office. As of the moment, reconciliation between two records have been made up to year 2015. Reconciliation for CY 2016 to current period is on going.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of all the property, plant and equipment of the Municipality and prepare and submit the required Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
We also recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their property records and compare the same with the Inventory Report in order to establish the correct balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. Any discrepancies noted between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs:
the Accountant to exert more effort to look for pertinent documents and records in order to justify the validity and lawfulness of the adjusting entries made. Supporting documents like a copy of the check, debit/credit memos, bank statements, check registers or any other records that will trace back these unaccounted amounts are very important in this matter as it involves the most liquid asset of the Municipality that is prone to fraud and misappropriation.
the Accounting and Treasury Office to continue their efforts in reconciling their records and closely coordinate with each other to finally resolve the said recurring issue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to request from the GSIS an updated statement of accounts of the Municipality so that they could settle immediately any outstanding liability with the System. If proven none, the Municipality may consider reverting back to the General Fund the said amount pursuant to existing rules and regulations on the matter.
Also, the Municipal Accountant to monitor strict compliance with the prescribed guidelines on remittances of withheld payroll deductions to avoid unnecessary penalty charge and also the interest that would be imposed on delayed remittances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instructs the BAC Secretariat to be compliant in posting procurement/bidding notices in the PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required by R.A. 9184 to attain the widest circulation of the agency’s procurement/bidding activities.
Instructs the Municipal officials concerned to submit all the cited required supporting documents. Henceforth, ensure that all procurement are properly supported with the  documents required under the  above regulations and  that only claims with complete documentation should be processed and paid by the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instructs the concerned official to comply strictly with any of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to facilitate validation of the same by the Audit Team and to promote transparency and accountability regarding the PPAs being undertaken by the Municipality. If necessary, appoint or assign somebody who will be responsible for the prompt preparation and submission of the required report/information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the concerned Municipal Officials to monitor the timely implementation of developmental projects, programs and activities and see to it that the 20% development funds are optimally utilized in accordance with the guidelines and for the project/program prescribed in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.s 2005 of the DILG and DBM as amended by Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated 13 April 2011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to:
Refrain from processing cash advances that would grant cash advance in excess of the maximum cash accountability allowed by the fidelity bond of the accountable officer;
Stop granting additional cash advances to accountable officers who have yet to liquidate their previous ones; and
See to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to be extra careful in recording the transaction of the Municipality to avoid inaccurate and misleading information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs: (a) the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with each other in order to immediately remit the unexpended balances of fund transfers to its source agencies as required under Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; (b) the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to record the transfers from PCSO to its appropriate account.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our
recommendation that the Municipal Government
intensify its effort to collect the long overdue
loan receivables to provide sufficient funds for
the continuing implementation of the
Municipal Government’s program of helping

Likewise, we recommended that specific and/or

observations.managementAction:
The Project Loan
Management Committee (PLMC) of the
Municipality conducted house to house visit to
the SEA-K beneficiaries and reported that from
the total 206 borrowers, 94 were victims and
survivor of Typhoon Ondoy and were observed
to be economically troubled up to this time.
Likewise, some of them could not pay their
obligation due to bankruptcy and some are
already deceased. The PMLC Head tried to
coordinate with listed cooperative
beneficiaries of the Livelihood Program and
found that only Sampaloc Agrarian Reform
Multi-Purpose Cooperative exist to date. A
meeting with the new set of officers was made
and agreed to settle their outstanding loan. An
initial payment was made in the amount of P
2,500.00. An on-going talk is being done to
settle the issue once and for all.
A letter dated March 15, 2017 was also sent to
the Audit Team by Dr. Herminigilda F.
Cabanting, MSWD Head, requesting relief from
accountability in behalf of the 94 borrowers
who are victim/survivors of Typhoon Ondoy and
3 deceased borrowers with a total loan balance
of P 166,990.00.

Furnished the team with the guidelines and
stopped for the meantime extension of loan to
farmers cooperatives.
